heading: Why trust this Las Vegas neighborhood guide
intro: This guide is built from real knowledge of Las Vegas's distinct zones — what staying on the Strip actually means for your daily experience versus Fremont Street or off-Strip, with honest cost comparisons.

items.title: Strip zones explained accurately
items.text: The Strip is not one area — mid, south, and north have meaningfully different experiences and price points. This guide explains each zone so you pick the right one for your trip.

items.title: Resort fees decoded
items.text: Every Strip area assessment includes the true nightly cost with resort fees — because $199/night plus $50 resort fee is $249/night, and that matters when comparing options.

items.title: Fremont vs Strip honestly compared
items.text: Fremont Street is genuinely worth considering for the right traveler — $40-80 cheaper per night, different atmosphere, older casino culture. This guide tells you honestly who it suits.

items.title: Off-Strip options included
items.text: Henderson and Summerlin are covered for visitors who want a quieter base — family travelers, those with outdoor plans, or longer stays where Strip energy would get exhausting.

footer: If you want to understand where to stay in Las Vegas honestly — what each area actually costs, feels like, and suits — this guide gives you the real picture.
